mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals impending economic contraction, triggering corporate earnings revisions downward and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the median 20-day forward return in triggered periods exceeds zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and tightening financial conditions erodes real disposable income and compresses valuation multiples, creating a stagflationary environment that drives negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a positive coefficient with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a supply-constrained regime indicates easing supply bottlenecks, which improves corporate input costs and profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases are followed by negative or flat S&P 500 returns in >60% of cases, or the rolling 1-year Spearman correlation between INDPRO changes and forward SPX returns is non-positive.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects robust consumer spending, which directly supports corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The rolling 1-year Rank IC of RSAFS changes against 20-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02, or forward returns in months with positive RSAFS growth are not statistically higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts below the 1500 thousand unit threshold signals weakening aggregate demand and potential credit market stress, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Housing starts below 1500 are followed by positive S&P 500 returns in >60% of instances, or the median 60-day forward return in triggered periods is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with a stable unemployment rate indicates a healthy labor market that sustains consumer demand without triggering aggressive inflationary wage spirals, supporting equity valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of observations, or a multivariate model shows the interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE stability has a negative coefficient with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, accelerating consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy longer than markets anticipate, compressing equity risk premiums and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: The joint CPI-FEDFUNDS signal produces positive 60-day forward returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the forward returns in high-inflation/tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
